rulings.Case Title: KEVIN STUCKART et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 5, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Union Carbide Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD HENDERSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 5, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff¿s motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: LINE 2, Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied. As to issues 1, 2 and 3 (punitive damages, false representation and intentional tort), defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting plaintiffs¿ false representation and intentional tort causes of action and claim for punitive damages. By failing to attach plaintiffs¿ verifications to defendant¿s contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Adjudication of issue 4 (loss of consortium) is denied pursuant to VanHooser v. Superior Court (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 921. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one reporter. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to B&P Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: LINE 2, Defendant Hill Brothers Chemical Co.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting their claim for punitive damages. By failing to attach plaintiffs¿ verifications to defendant¿s contention discovery that attempted to elicit information about punitive damages (Set Two) with its moving papers, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Plaintiffs¿ objections to defendant¿s attempt to submit those verifications with its reply are sustained. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one reporter. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to B&P Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: Line 2. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s MSA of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories identified Coy Yancy as a person with facts supporting plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages (no. 32), provided his address and defendant¿s moving papers failed to demonstrate that Mr. Yancy did not know facts supporting plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages. Defendant¿s special interrogatories did not ask what facts or knowledge persons identified in response to that interrogatory had. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A reporter will not be provided by the court. The parties must meet and confer to agree on only one reporter for any reported matter in this case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to B&P Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree, the court will designate a qualified reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the costs. Any party who contests a tentative must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative and must bring it to the hearing. If a tentative is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order and must either bring it to the hearing or submit after the hearing in compliance with CRC3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
